svg.text: How calculate sinus cosinus and tangente for k * A For k = 2, we can use additive formula with A = B: cos ( 2 * A ) = cos ( A ) * cos ( A ) - sin ( A ) * sin ( A ) cos ( 2 * A ) = cos2 ( A ) - sin2 ( A ) cos ( 2 * A ) = 1 - 2 * sin2 ( A ) cos ( 2 * A ) = 2 * cos2 ( A ) - 1 sin ( 2 * A ) = sin ( A ) * cos ( A ) + sin ( A ) * cos ( A ) sin ( 2 * A ) = 2 * sin ( A ) * cos ( A ) tan ( 2 * A ) = ( tan ( A ) * tan ( A ) ) / ( 1 - tan ( A ) * tan ( A ) ) tan ( 2 * A ) = 2 * tan ( A ) / ( 1 - tan2 ( A ) ) We can continue with formulas but it's easier to use complex number : z = cos ( A ) + i * sin ( A ) zn = cos ( n * A ) + i * sin ( n * A ) We use formula to develop z3 by example z3 = cos3 ( A ) + 3 * i * cos2 ( A ) * sin ( A ) + 3 * i2 * cos ( A ) * sin2 ( A ) + i3 * sin3 ( A ) sin ( 3 * A ) = 3 * cos2 ( A ) * sin ( A ) - sin3 ( A ) cos ( 3 * A ) = cos3 ( A ) - 3 * cos ( A ) * sin2 ( A ) For tan ( 3 * A ), we divise all terms by cos3 ( A ) tan ( 3 * A ) = ( 3 * tan ( A ) - tan3 ( A ) ) / ( 1 - 3 * tan3 ( A ) ) With same method we have : sin ( 4 * A ) = 4 * cos3 ( A ) * sin ( A ) - 4 * cos ( A ) * sin3 ( A ) cos ( 4 * A ) = cos4 ( A ) - 6 * cos2 ( A ) * sin2 ( A ) + sin4 ( A )
